title: Dense Herbal Norwegian Lamb Stew
description: A hearty and flavorful Norwegian stew made with lamb and a blend of herbs, featuring allspice and kombu-cold-extract for added depth, this dish is paleo-friendly and perfect for a cold winter night.
instructions: Heat a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat and brown the lamb cubes on all sides, then set them aside.
Add the chopped onion to the pot and cook until it is lightly browned, about 5 minutes.
Add the chopped carrots and celery to the pot and cook for an additional 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add the minced garlic to the pot and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Add the browned lamb, allspice, kombu-cold-extract, thyme, and rosemary to the pot and stir to combine.
Pour in the beef broth and bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 2.5 hours, or until the lamb is tender.
Season the stew with salt and pepper to taste, then serve hot.
Garnish with fresh herbs, if desired.
